title: The One With Five Steaks and an Eggplant

lines.text: Originally written by Chris Brown
At Chandler and Joey's. Ross and Chandler are there. Ross is watching wrestling.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Man, I sure miss Julie.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Spanish midgets. Spanish midgets wrestling. Julie. Ok, yes, I see how you got there. (phone rings)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You ever figure out what that thing's for?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, see, I'm trying this new screening thing. You know, I figure if I'm always answering the phone, people'll think I don't have a life. My god, Rodrigo never gets pinned.
MACHINE--JOEY'S VOICE): Here comes the beep, you know what to do.

lines.speaker: Jade
lines.text: Hello, I'm looking for Bob. This is Jane. I don't know if you're still at this number, but I was just thinking about us, and how great it was, and, well, I know it's been three years, but, I was kinda hoping we could hook up again. I barely had t he nerve to make this call, so you know what I did?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Jade
lines.text: I got a little drunk...and naked.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Bob here.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (on phone) What've you been up to?

lines.speaker: Jade
lines.text: Oh, you know, the usual, teaching aerobics, partying way too much. Oh, and in case you were wondering, those are my legs on the new James Bond poster.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Can you hold on a moment? I have another call. (to Ross) I love her.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I know.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm back.

lines.speaker: Jade
lines.text: So, are we gonna get together or what?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Um, absolutely. Uh, how 'bout tomorrow afternoon? Do you know uh, Central Perk in the Village, say, five-ish?

lines.speaker: Jade
lines.text: Great, I'll see you then.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ok. Ok. Having a phone has finally paid off.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Even though you do do a good Bob impression, I'm thinkin' when she sees you tomorow, she's probably gonna realize, "hey, you're not Bob."

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm hoping that when Bob doesn't show up, she will seek comfort in the open arms of the wry stranger at the next table.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh my god. You are pure evil.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ok, pure evil, horny and alone. I've done this.
At Monica and Rachel's

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (on phone) Yeah, yeah, everybody's here. Hey, everybody, say hi to Julie in New Mexico.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hi, Julie!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (sarcastically) Hi, Julie.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ok, while Ross is on the phone, everybody owes me 62 bucks for his birthday.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Um, is, is there any chance that you're rounding up? You know, like from, like 20?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, come on, we got the gift, the concert, and the cake.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Do we need a cake?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look guys, I know it's a little steep.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, whoosh!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: But it's Ross.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It's Ross.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'll see you guys later, I gotta go...do a thing.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ok, sweetheart, I'll call you later tonight. Whoa, whoa, whoa, hey, hey, hey, you're not really gonna go through with this, are you?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You know, I think I might just.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So uh, what are you guys doing for dinner tonight?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well I guess I gotta start savin' up for Ross's birthday, so I guess I'll just stay home and eat dust bunnies.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Can you believe how much this is gonna cost?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Do you guys ever get the feeling that um, Chandler and those guys just don't get that we don't make as much money as they do?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yes! Yeah, it's like they're always saying "let's go here, let's go there". Like we can afford to go here and there.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes, yes, and it's, and we always have to go to, you know, someplace nice, you know? God, and it's not like we can say anything about it, 'cause, like this birthday thing, it's for Ross.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: For Ross.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: For Ross, Ross, Ross.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (enters) Oh my god.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm at work, ordinary day, you know, chop chop chop, sauti, sauti, sauti. All of a sudden, Leon, the manager, calls me into his office. It turns out they fired the head lunch chef, and guess who got the job.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: If it's not you, this is a horrible story.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Fortunately, it is me. And, they made me head of purchasing, thank you very much. Anyway, I just ran into Ross and Chandler downstairs, and they think we should go out and celebrate. You know, someplace nice.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, someplace nice. (to Phoebe and Rachel) How much do you think I can get for my kidney?
at Central Perk

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm tellin' you. You can't do this.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, come on. I can never get a girl like that with conventional methods.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That doesn't matter. She wanted to call Bob. Hey, for all we know, Bob is who she was meant to be with. You may be destroying two people's chance for happiness.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We don't know Bob, ok? We know me. We like me. Please let me be happy.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Go over there and tell that woman the truth.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Go.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Jade
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Listen, I have to, uh, um, I have to, I have to confess something.

lines.speaker: Jade
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Whoever stood you up is a jerk.

lines.speaker: Jade
lines.text: How did you--?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don't know. I just had this weird sense. You know, but that's me. I'm weird and sensitive. Tissue?

lines.speaker: Jade
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, you keep the pack. I'm all cried out today.
At Somplace Nice

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ok, ok, here is to my sister, the newly-appointed head lunch chef--

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Who is also in charge of purchasing.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Newly appointed head lunch chef who is also in charge of purchasing--

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Who has her own little desk when Roland's not there.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, lunch chef, purchasing, own little desk when Roland's not there. Here's to my little sister--

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, wait, and I got a beeper!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Cool.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Let's see!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's fine, I'll just wait!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, sorry.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Sorry, sorry.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Monica!
glasses clinking

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: Are we ready to order?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, you know what, we haven't even looked yet.

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: Well, when you do, just let me know. I'll be right over there on the edge of my seat.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wow, look at these prices.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, these are pretty ch-ching.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What are these, like famous chickens?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, sorry I'm late. Congratulations, Mon. (to Ross) I'm not sorry I'm late. How incredible was my afternoon with Jade?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, pretty incredible according to the message she left you on my machine. Hey, Chandler, why is this woman leaving a message for you on my machine?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, see, I had to tell her that my number was your number, because I couldn't tell her that my number was my number because she thinks that my number is Bob's number.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, tell me again, what do I do when Mr. Roper calls?

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: Do I dare ask?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes, I will start with the carpaccio, and then I'll have the grilled prawns.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That sounds great. Same for me.

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: And for the gentleman?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, I'll have the Thai chicken pizza. But, hey, look, if I get it without the nuts and leeks and stuff, is it cheaper?

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: You'd think, wouldn't you? Miss?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ok, I will have the uh, (whispers) side salad.

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: (whispers) And what will that be on the side of?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh, I don't know. Why don't you put it right here next to my water?

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: And for you?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Um, I'm gonna have a cup of the cucumber soup, and, um, take care.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I will have the uh, Cajun catfish.

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.text: Anything else?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, how 'bout a verse of Killing Me Softly. You're gonna sneeze on my fish, aren't you?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (using calculator) Plus tip, divided by six. Ok, everyone owes 28 bucks.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Um, everyone?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, you're right, I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Monica's big night, she shouldn't pay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, thank you!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So five of us is, $33.50 apiece.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, huh uh, no way, I'm sorry, not gonna happen.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Whoa, whoa, prom night flashback.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm sorry, Monica, I'm really happy you got promoted, but cold cucumber mush for thirty-something bucks? No! Rachel just had that, that, that salad, and, and Joey with his like teeny pizza! It's just...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ok, Pheebs! How 'bout we'll each just pay for what we had. It's no big deal.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Not for you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, what's goin' on?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ok, look you guys, I really don't want to get into this right now. I think it'll just make everyone uncomfortable.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Fine. All right, fine.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You can tell us.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hello, it's us, all right? It'll be fine.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ok, um, uh, we three feel like, that uh, sometimes you guys don't get that uh, we don't have as much money as you.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ok.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I hear ya.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We can talk about that.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, then...Let's.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I, I just never think of money as an issue.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That's 'cause you have it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's a good point.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So um, how come you guys haven't talked about this before?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: 'Cause it's always somethin', you know, like Monica's new job, or the whole Ross's birthday hoopla.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wha--? Whoa, hey, I don't want my birthday to be the source of any kind of negative--there's gonna be a hoopla?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Basically, there's the thing, and then there's the stuff after the thing.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: If it makes anybody feel better, then we can just forget the thing, and we'll just do the gift.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: G-gift? The thing's not the gift?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, the thing was, we were gonna go see Hootie and the Blowfish.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hootie and the--oh my. I, I can catch them on the radio.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, now I feel bad. You wanna go to the concert.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, look, hey, it's my birthday, and the important thing is that we all be together.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All of us.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Together.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Not at the concert.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ok.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So, the ebola virus. That's gotta suck, huh?
at Monica and Rachel's

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Gee, Monica, what's in the bag?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don't know, Chandler. Let's take a look.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, it's like a skit.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Why, it's dinner for six. 5 steaks, and an eggplant for Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Whoo!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Cool.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, we switched meat suppliers at work, and the new guys gave me the steaks as sort of a thank-you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: But wait, there's more. Hey, Chandler, what is in the envelope?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: By the way, this didn't seem so dorky in the hall.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Come on.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why, it's six tickets to Hootie and the Blowfish! The Blowfish!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's on us, all right, so don't worry. It's our treat.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So...Thank you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Could you be less enthused?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look, it's a nice gesture, it is. But it just feels like--

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Like?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Charity.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Charity?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: We're just tryin' to do a nice thing here.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, you have to understand that your nice thing makes us feel this big.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Actually, it makes us feel that big.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I don't, I don't understand. I mean, you, it's like we can't win with you guys.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: If you guys feel this big, maybe that's not our fault. Maybe that's just how you feel.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, now you're tellin' us how you feel.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ok, we never shoulda talked about this.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm just gonna pass on the concert, 'cause I'm just not in a very Hootie place right now.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Me neither.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Me too.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Guys, we bought the tickets.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, well, then you'll have extra seats, you know, for all your tiaras and stuff.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why did you look at me when you said that?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, I guess now we can't go.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What? Come on, you do what you want to do. Do we always have to do everything together?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You know what? You're right.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Fine.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Fine.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Fine.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Fine.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Fine.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right. We're gonna go. It's not for another six hours. We're gonna go then.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Chandler!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Geez! Are you ready?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah. Just let me grab my jacket and tell you I had sex today.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Whoa! You had sex today?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wow, it sounds even cooler when somebody else says it. I was awesome, ok? She was biting her lip to stop from screaming.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Now I know it's been awhile, but I took it as a good sign.
phone rings

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Still doing the screening thing?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I had sex today. I never have to answer that phone again.

lines.speaker: Machine
lines.text: Here comes the beep, you know what to do.

lines.speaker: Jade
lines.text: Hey, Bob, it's Jade. Listen, I just wanted to tell you that I was really hurt when you didn't show up the other day, and just so you know, I ended up meeting a guy.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Bob here.

lines.speaker: Jade
lines.text: Oh, hi.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So, uh, you met someone, huh?

lines.speaker: Jade
lines.text: Yes, yes, I did. In fact, I had sex with him 2 hours ago.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So, uh, how was he?

lines.speaker: Jade
lines.text: Eh.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Eh?

lines.speaker: Jade
lines.text: Oh, Bob, he was nothing compared to you. I had to bite my lip to keep from screaming your name.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, that makes me feel so good.

lines.speaker: Jade
lines.text: It was just so awkward and bumpy.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (silently mouthing) Bumpy?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, maybe he had some kind of uh, new, cool style, that you're not familiar with. And uh maybe you have to get used to it.

lines.speaker: Jade
lines.text: Well there really wasn't much time to get used to it, you know what I mean?
at the concert

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You know what? I'm not gonna be able to enjoy this.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, I know, it's my birthday. We all should be here.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So, let's go.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well maybe, you know, maybe we should stay for one song.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, I mean, it would be rude to them for us to leave now.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You know, the guys are probably having a great time.
at Monica and Rachel's

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Come on you guys, one more time.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ok. One.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Nooo.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That was amazing!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Excellent, that was excellent.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I can't believe the guys missed this.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What guys? Oh, yeah.

lines.speaker: Steve
lines.text: Excuse me, you're Monica Geller aren't you?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Do I know you?

lines.speaker: Steve
lines.text: You used to be my babysitter.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my god, little Stevie Fisher? How've you been?

lines.speaker: Steve
lines.text: Good, good, I'm a lawyer now.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You can't be a lawyer. You're eight.

lines.speaker: Steve
lines.text: Listen, it was nice to see you. I gotta run backstage.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Uh, wait, backstage?

lines.speaker: Steve
lines.text: Oh, yeah, my firm represents the band.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ross.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Chandler.

lines.speaker: Steve
lines.text: How are you? Look, you guys wanna meet the group? Come on. So, are you one of the ones who fooled around with my dad?
at Central Perk

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, you guys.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Happy birthday.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, thank you, thanks. So uh, how was your night last night?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, well, it pretty much sucked. How was yours?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, ours pretty much sucked, oh, but, I did run into little Stevie Fisher. Remember him?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah. I used to babysit him. Hey, how's his dad?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Uh, good.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, aside from that, the whole evening was pretty much a bust.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, we really missed you guys.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, look, we were just saying, this whole thing is really stupid.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: We just have to really, really, really, not let stuff like money get--is that a hickey?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, I just, I fell down.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: On someone's lips? Where'd you get the hickey?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You know, a party, or--

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What party?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It wasn't so much a party as...a gathering of people, with food, and music, and, and the band.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You partied with Hootie and the Blowfish?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, apparently Stevie and the band are like this.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Who gave you that hickey?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That would be the work of a Blowfish.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh! I can't believe it. I can't believe this. We're just like, sitting at home, trying to guess Joey's fingers, and you guys are out like partying and having fun, and you know, all, "hey, Blowfish, suck on my neck".

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, don't blame us. You guys coulda been there, you know.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What, as part of your poor friends outreach program?
Monica's pager goes off

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's work.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don't know what to say. I'm sorry that we make more money than you. But we're not gonna feel guilty about it. We work really hard for it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And we don't work hard?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (on phone) Yeah, hi, it's Monica. I just got a page.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm just saying that sometimes we like to do stuff that costs a little more.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And you feel like we hold you back.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Leon, Leon. Shhh! Guys. Wait, I don't understand. Those steaks were just a gift from the meat vendor. That was not a kick back. I'll just replace them and we can forget the whole thing. What corporate policy? No. Yeah. All right. I just got fired.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh.
Everyone goes over to comfort Monica

lines.speaker: Waitress
lines.text: Here's your check. That'll be $4.12.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Let me get that. (to Chandler) You got five bucks?

lines.speaker: Machine
lines.text: Here comes the beep, you know what to do.

lines.speaker: Jade
lines.text: Hi, it's me. Listen, Bob. I'm probably way out of line here. I mean, It has been 3 years, and you're probably seeing someone else now, but if we could just have one night together, just for old time's sake, one hot, steamy, wild night...
Joey lunges for phone and misses.
END
